elements.sent-2411-001: Our universe is full of mysteries.
elements.sent-2411-002: The presence of a non-luminous matter component, known as Dark Matter (DM) \cite{Zwicky:1937zza, Zwicky:1933gu is one of them.}
elements.sent-2411-003: Different kinds of astrophysical and cosmological observations, like Galaxy rotation curves \cite{Sofue:2000jx , Bullet Cluster \cite{Hayashi:2006kw , CMBR anisotropic spectrum \cite{Komatsu:2014ioa suggest that DM is present and possesses 26.8\% of the energy budget of the Universe and this gives rise to one of the most important observables related to DM, called the relic density, expressed as $\rm\Omega_{DM h^2=0.1200\pm 0.0012$ \cite{Planck:2018vyg , where $\Omega=\rho/\rho_c$ is cosmological density and $h$ is reduced Hubble constant.}}}}}
elements.sent-2411-004: What constitutes the DM is a key question, which has involved a lot of researches and possibilities so far.
elements.sent-2411-005: Particle DM has been very popular since it can judiciously explain almost all the observations related to DM.
elements.sent-2411-006: However, none of the observations can pinpoint what kind of particle it is, although some broad characteristics like electromagnetic charge neutrality, massiveness and stability at the scale of Universe's life time are assigned to them.
elements.sent-2411-007: Further debate erupts on the question whether the DM is composed of a single type of particle or have different particles of different nature, adhering to the broad characteristics of DM.
elements.sent-2411-008: Again, the possibilities here are infinite and the only relevant constraint comes from the Bullet cluster observation, which restricts the DM-DM self-interaction \cite{Clowe:2006eq .}
elements.sent-2411-009: One thing we know for sure, that Standard Model (SM) of particle physics do not contain any DM and requires extension to accommodate one (or more) DM, protected by a symmetry different from that of SM gauge symmetries.
elements.sent-2411-010: DM characteristics mainly depend on its production mechanism.
elements.sent-2411-011: There are two possibilities (broadly) depending on whether it was in thermal bath during reheating or not.
elements.sent-2411-012: Those which were in equilibrium with bath and freezes out at some point with expanding universe are called thermal DM, and those which were not in equilibrium and produced via out-of equilibrium processes to freeze in, are called non-thermal ones.
elements.sent-2411-013: The most popular thermal DM candidates are weakly interacting massive particle (WIMP)\cite{Roszkowski:2017nbc,Kamionkowski:1990ni, Gondolo:1990dk,Jungman:1995df,Edsjo:1997bg,Bottino:1993zx and strongly interacting massive particle (SIMP) \cite{Hochberg:2014dra, Hochberg:2014kqa, Hochberg:2015vrg, Tulin:2017ara, Choi:2018iit, Bhattacharya:2019mmy, Barman:2021ugy, Kamada:2022zwb , while a well studied non-thermal possibility gives rise to feebly interacting massive particle (FIMP) \cite{Hall:2009bx,Yaguna:2011qn, Bernal:2017kxu, DEramo:2020gpr,Chakrabarty:2022bcn, Biswas:2016iyh type of DM.}}}
elements.sent-2411-014: Multipartite DM can be constituted by any combination of them.
elements.sent-2411-015: The most studied frameworks involve similar DM components, like WIMP-WIMP \cite{Bhattacharya:2013hva, Bhattacharya:2016ysw , FIMP-FIMP \cite{Pandey:2017quk, PeymanZakeri:2018zaa , and SIMP-SIMP \cite{Ho:2022erb, Choi:2021yps .}}}
elements.sent-2411-016: WIMP-FIMP \cite{DuttaBanik:2016jzv, Bhattacharya:2021rwh combination is possible when the interaction between DM components is also feeble.}
elements.sent-2411-017: However, if we have sizeable interaction between the DM components, then it gives rise to an interesting possibility of a pseudo FIMP (pFIMP) \cite{Bhattacharya:2022dco, Bhattacharya:2022vxm,Bhattacharya:2024nla type DM.}
elements.sent-2411-018: How ? Then FIMP, in spite of having feeble interaction with the visible sector, reaches thermal equilibrium thanks to the large conversion with the WIMP partner, and is called pseudo-FIMP (pFIMP).
elements.sent-2411-019: However the freeze-out characteristics depend on the WIMP partner, making it a special/different DM component.
elements.sent-2411-020: We wish to take the study of pFIMP one step ahead.
elements.sent-2411-021: We show here how pFIMP can be realised in presence of a SIMP like DM component.
elements.sent-2411-022: The freeze out characteristics of pFIMP then depends very much on those of its SIMP partner.
elements.sent-2411-023: We establish this in a model independent analysis via solving coupled Boltzmann Equations, so that it is applicable to any kind of pFIMP-SIMP combination, immaterial to their particle properties.
elements.sent-2411-024: Second, we establish the same via a concrete model example involving two scalar fields, one real, and the other complex, stabilized by $\mathbb{Z _2\otimes \mathbb{Z _3$ symmetry.}}
elements.sent-2411-025: This is arguably the simplest scenario yielding pFIMP-SIMP model.
elements.sent-2411-026: The search strategies for DM known so far, like direct detection (DD), indirect detection (ID), or collider searches are difficult to reveal such DM combinations unless the SIMP is accessible to the detector via light mediator.
elements.sent-2411-027: This article is organized as follows: sec\,.~\ref{sec:2 provides a brief discussion on a single component SIMP, sec\,.~\ref{sec:3 explores the dynamics and phenomenology of pFIMP in a model-independent manner, and sec\,.~\ref{mod:-simp-pfimp presents a model example.}}}

elements.section-2411-003: pFIMP in presence of a SIMP: model independent analysis
elements.sent-2411-071: Let us briefly recapitulate the essence of pFIMP \cite{Bhattacharya:2022dco, Bhattacharya:2022vxm first.}
elements.sent-2411-072: The existence of pFIMP was demonstrated in presence of WIMP in \cite{Bhattacharya:2022dco, Bhattacharya:2022vxm .}
elements.sent-2411-073: Here we show that SIMP being another kind of thermal DM, can accommodate a pFIMP DM in a multicomponent set up (the second possibility in tab\,.~\ref{tab:simp-scenrio ), with substantial interactions between them.}
elements.sent-2411-074: The analysis has been done in two steps: firstly, a model-independent analysis on the pFIMP-SIMP scenario, in sec\,.~\ref{sec:3 , showing that the pFIMP dynamics is possible in the presence of a SIMP.}
elements.sent-2411-075: Secondly, we do the same exercise for the simplest two-component real (pFIMP) and complex scalar SIMP DM model, in sec\,.~\ref{mod:-simp-pfimp .}

elements.sent-2411-193: Out of the DM masses, self couplings, portal couplings, and conversion couplings, the conversion coupling $\lambda_{\chi\phi}$ helps the pFIMP reach thermal equilibrium.
elements.sent-2411-194: The parameter space is crucially constrained by the self interaction bounds.
elements.sent-2411-195: Interestingly, couplings like $\rm \lambda_{\phi ,~and~\lambda_{\chi H}$, which play insignificant role in DM freeze out, gets constrained by DM self-interaction.}
elements.sent-2411-196: As usual, DM mass splittings get constrained to yield pFIMP solution, but different depending on mass hierarchy.
elements.sent-2411-197: For example, $\Delta m \lesssim \rm 2~MeV$ for heavier SIMP, and $\Delta m\lesssim \rm 100~MeV$ when pFIMP is heavier.
elements.sent-2411-198: Also, relic density allowed parameter space of this model surpasses the single component SIMP allowed range, in particular, the SIMP mass is allowed up to $\sim 50$ MeV when $m_{\phi >m_{\chi}$.}
elements.sent-2411-199: Detectability of pFIMP-SMIP model is difficult as the portal coupling of both pFIMP and SIMP with SM Higgs is suppressed to address their freeze outs.
elements.sent-2411-200: Secondly, their interaction with SM is mediated by SM Higgs, which is way heavier than the DM mass ($\sim$MeV), making the cross-section further subdued.
elements.sent-2411-201: We thus propose a minimal extension of the dark sector by the inclusion of a charged vector-like lepton.
elements.sent-2411-202: This is primarily assumed to interact with electrons only, to avoid conflicts with lepton flavor violation constraints.
elements.sent-2411-203: This provides a different mediator to interact with direct or indirect searches.
elements.sent-2411-204: The presence of such new Yukawa interaction terms opens up direct (via electron scattering) and indirect (annihilation into electron and photon pair) search prospects of the pFIMP-SIMP scenario.

elements.sent-2411-215: In single component complex scalar SIMP scenario, The effective DM self-scattering cross-section over mass is written as \cite{Choi:2015bya , \begin{equation}\frac{\sigma_{\rm self}}{m_{\rm DM}}=\frac{1}{m_{\chi}}\left(\dfrac{\Omega^2_{\chi}}{\Omega_{\rm DM}^2}\sigma_{\chi\chi\to\chi\chi}+\dfrac{\Omega^2_{\chi^*}}{\Omega_{\rm DM}^2}\sigma_{\chi^{*}\chi^{*}\to\chi^{*}\chi^{*}}+\dfrac{\Omega_{\chi}}{\Omega_{\rm DM}}\dfrac{\Omega_{\chi^*}}{\Omega_{\rm DM}}\sigma_{\chi\chi^{*}\to\chi\chi^{*}}\right)\,. \label{eq:simp_dm-self}\end{equation}.}

elements.sent-2411-218: Only relativistic fermions are available during SIMP freezes-out, and contributing to kinetic decoupling condition is written regarding DM-SM scattering, fig\,.~\ref{fig:26a , rate as, \bea 2\sum_{f}\Gamma_{\chi f\to \chi\to f =2\sum_{f}\langle \sigma v\rangle_{\chi f\to \chi f n_f^{\rm eq (T)\gtrsim \mathcal{H (T)\,.. \eea where, \begin{equation}\langle \sigma v\rangle_{\chi f\to\chi f}=\frac{\int_{(m_{\chi}+m_{f})^2}^{\infty}\sigma_{\chi f\to\chi f}K_1\left(\frac{\sqrt{s}}{T}\right)\sqrt{s-(m_{\chi}-m_f)^2}\left(s-(m_{\chi}+m_{f})^2\right)ds}{8Tm_{\chi}^2m_{f}^2K_2\left(\frac{m_{\chi}}{T}\right)K_2\left(\frac{m_{f}}{T}\right)}\,.\end{equation}.}}}}}

elements.sent-2411-219: Fig\,.~\ref{fig:kint-decup represents the evolution of DM-SM elastic scattering rate with the SM bath temperature $(T)$.}
elements.sent-2411-220: Our work focuses on sub-MeV DMs and suitable choice of $\lambda_{\chi H}\sim 0.1$, without affecting DM relic, to achieve kinetic equilibrium between DM and SM bath.
elements.sent-2411-221: The kinetic equilibriation, with thermal bath, of $\chi$ ensures the pFIMP ($\phi$) kinetic equilibriation also because of the presence of strong self-interaction among themselves.
elements.sent-2411-222: For this reason, there is no necessity to do similar kinds of analysis for $\phi$.. \bibliographystyle{JHEP.}
